content.text: Zero-shot {{c1::prompting}} asks the model to perform a task without examples, while few-shot prompting provides a small set of exemplars.
content.explanation: The choice of prompting style strongly affects accuracy, consistency, and cost.
content.spanish: El prompting zero-shot pide al modelo que haga una tarea sin ejemplos, mientras que few-shot proporciona un pequeño conjunto de ejemplares.
content.usage: Common in classification, extraction, and transformation tasks.

content.text: Chain-of-thought {{c1::prompting}} encourages the model to break a problem into intermediate reasoning steps.
content.explanation: This can improve performance on arithmetic and symbolic tasks, though it can also increase cost and verbosity.
content.spanish: El prompting de chain-of-thought anima al modelo a descomponer un problema en pasos intermedios de razonamiento.
content.usage: Useful in reasoning-heavy workflows and complex planning tasks.

content.text: Self-{{c1::consistency}} samples multiple reasoning paths and chooses the most frequent answer.
content.explanation: It is a robust technique for improving reliability on multi-step tasks.
content.spanish: La auto-consistencia toma múltiples caminos de razonamiento y elige la respuesta más frecuente.
content.usage: Effective for math, logic, and constrained planning problems.

content.text: {{c1::Exact Match}} measures whether the predicted answer matches the reference exactly.
content.explanation: It is strict and useful for structured outputs, but it can underestimate semantically correct answers.
content.spanish: Exact Match mide si la respuesta predicha coincide exactamente con la referencia.
content.usage: Common in QA benchmarks and extraction tasks.

content.text: BLEU and {{c1::ROUGE}} are classic text-generation metrics that compare overlap with reference outputs.
content.explanation: They are useful but imperfect for measuring fluency, faithfulness, and usefulness.
content.spanish: BLEU y ROUGE son métricas clásicas de generación de texto que comparan solapamiento con salidas de referencia.
content.usage: Applied in summarization, translation, and retrieval evaluation.

content.text: nDCG evaluates {{c1::ranking}} quality by rewarding relevant documents higher in the result list.
content.explanation: It is especially useful for search, recommendation, and retrieval systems.
content.spanish: nDCG evalúa la calidad del ranking premiando documentos relevantes más arriba en la lista de resultados.
content.usage: Common in search engines and semantic retrieval evaluation.

content.text: {{c1::Hallucination detection}} checks whether an answer is supported by the available evidence or context.
content.explanation: A strong evaluation pipeline must assess factual grounding, not only style.
content.spanish: La detección de alucinaciones revisa si una respuesta está sustentada por la evidencia o contexto disponible.
content.usage: Essential for knowledge assistants, copilots, and semantic search systems.

content.text: Structured {{c1::outputs}} use schemas like JSON or XML to ensure predictable, machine-readable responses.
content.explanation: This is now standard in tool calling, agents, and pipeline integrations.
content.spanish: Los outputs estructurados usan esquemas como JSON o XML para asegurar respuestas predecibles y legibles por máquina.
content.usage: Critical for automation, extraction, and RAG-backed applications.

content.text: {{c1::Task success}} measures whether the agent completed the intended objective, not merely whether it produced fluent text.
content.explanation: It is the most important metric for production agent workflows.
content.spanish: El task success mide si el agente completó el objetivo previsto, no solo si produjo texto fluido.
content.usage: Used in coding agents, support agents, and automated workflow systems.

content.text: {{c1::Tool}} call accuracy measures whether the agent selected and used the correct tool at the right time.
content.explanation: It is essential for tool-using and action-oriented agents.
content.spanish: La precisión de tool calls mide si el agente seleccionó y usó la herramienta correcta en el momento adecuado.
content.usage: Critical in search, database, and coding assistants.

content.text: {{c1::Hallucination rate}} tracks how often an agent makes unsupported claims or invents facts.
content.explanation: It is one of the most important safety and trust metrics for agents.
content.spanish: La tasa de alucinación mide cuán a menudo un agente hace afirmaciones no sustentadas o inventa hechos.
content.usage: Critical for knowledge agents, legal assistants, and enterprise copilots.

content.text: {{c1::Trace logging}} captures the full path of an agent run, including prompts, tool calls, intermediate outputs, and final decisions.
content.explanation: Without traces, debugging agent behavior becomes guesswork.
content.spanish: El trace logging captura el camino completo de una ejecución de agente, incluyendo prompts, tool calls, outputs intermedios y decisiones finales.
content.usage: Essential in production agent orchestration and debugging.

content.text: {{c1::Failure modes}} include loops, wrong tool selection, premature termination, and policy violations.
content.explanation: Classifying failures makes it possible to improve reliability systematically.
content.spanish: Los failure modes incluyen bucles, selección incorrecta de herramientas, terminación prematura y violaciones de política.
content.usage: Used in agent benchmarking, safety reviews, and incident analysis.

content.text: {{c1::Human}}-in-the-loop systems ask for approval or intervention when the agent is uncertain, risky, or high-impact.
content.explanation: It is a practical safeguard for enterprise deployment.
content.spanish: Los sistemas human-in-the-loop piden aprobación o intervención cuando el agente está incierto, es riesgoso o de alto impacto.
content.usage: Used in finance, healthcare, and customer support operations.

content.explanation:
Desglose de variables:
  • \(n\): Total number of generated code samples per problem.
  • \(c\): Number of correct generated code samples that pass unit tests.
  • \(k\): Number of samples selected to determine success probability.
  • \(\binom{\cdot}{\cdot}\): Binomial coefficient.
content.spanish: Fórmula de pass@k Coding Evaluation Formula

content.text:
Pregunta: What is the core execution mechanism of SWE-bench (Jimenez et al., 2024) to evaluate code generation agents?
Opciones:
  • A) Running agent-generated code patches within isolated Docker containers containing repository states and verifying unit test execution success
  • B) Comparing generated python strings using token-matching BLEU scores
  • C) Statically parsing agent ASTs to verify syntax validation rules
  • D) Prompting Claude to evaluate agent code on a scale of 1 to 10

Respuesta correcta: {{c1::A) Running agent-generated code patches within isolated Docker containers containing repository states and verifying unit test execution success}}
content.explanation: Justificación: SWE-bench evaluates agent patches by executing them inside specialized Docker environments containing the exact codebase state. It runs pre-existing unit tests to check if the patch resolves the targeted GitHub issue without breaking other functionalities.
content.spanish: Quiz: What is the core execution mechanism of SWE-bench (Jimenez et al., 2024) to evaluate code generation agents?
content.usage: Quiz assertion: A) Running agent-generated code patches within isolated Docker containers containing repository states and verifying unit test execution success

content.text:
Pregunta: Which set of systematic biases must be mitigated when using an LLM as a judge to evaluate pairwise model generations?
Opciones:
  • A) Position bias (order effects), Verbosity bias (favoring longer outputs), and Self-enhancement bias
  • B) Quantization noise, stochastic token dropouts, and beam search decay
  • C) Underfitting bias, covariate distribution shift, and validation leakages
  • D) Temperature scale differences, context window decay, and tokenizer compression loss

Respuesta correcta: {{c1::A) Position bias (order effects), Verbosity bias (favoring longer outputs), and Self-enhancement bias}}
content.explanation: Justificación: LLMs judging pairwise outputs suffer from Position bias (preferring whichever output is presented first), Verbosity bias (giving higher scores to longer, more detailed-looking responses even if they contain errors), and Self-enhancement bias (preferring responses generated by their own model weights). Mitigation includes swapping order and averaging evaluations.
content.spanish: Quiz: Which set of systematic biases must be mitigated when using an LLM as a judge to evaluate pairwise model generations?
content.usage: Quiz assertion: A) Position bias (order effects), Verbosity bias (favoring longer outputs), and Self-enhancement bias
